words.definition: A practice of physical, mental, and spiritual exercises.
words.definitionUz: Yoga — jismoniy, ruhiy va ma’naviy mashqlardan iborat amaliyot.
words.example1: She practices yoga every morning.
words.example1Translation: U har kuni ertalab yoga bilan shug‘ullanadi.
extraExamples: He started yoga for better health.
The yoga teacher is very experienced.
Yoga can be done at home.
Breathing exercises are part of yoga.
extraExampleTranslations: U sog‘liqni yaxshilash uchun yogani boshladi.
Nafas olish mashqlari yoganing bir qismi.

words.definition: A practice of focusing the mind to achieve calm and clarity.
words.definitionUz: Meditatsiya — ongni tinchlantirish va aniqlikka erishish uchun diqqatni jamlagan amaliyot.
words.example1: She practices meditation daily.
words.example1Translation: U har kuni meditatsiya bilan shug‘ullanadi.
words.example3: Many people use meditation for relaxation.
words.example3Translation: Ko‘p odamlar meditatsiyadan dam olish uchun foydalanadi.
extraExamples: Morning meditation sets a good tone for the day.
Meditation is part of many spiritual practices.
The meditation class was very peaceful.
extraExampleTranslations: Ertalabki meditatsiya kunni yaxshi boshlashga yordam beradi.
Meditatsiya ko‘p ma’naviy amaliyotlarning bir qismi.

words.definition: The way a person lives.
words.definitionUz: Turmush tarzi — odamning yashash usuli.
words.example1: She has a healthy lifestyle.
words.example3Translation: Turmush tarzini o‘zgartirish salomatlikni yaxshilashi mumkin.
extraExampleTranslations: Turmush tarzi tanlovlari farovonlikka ta’sir qiladi.
Shahar turmush tarzi qishloqdan farq qiladi.
U yangi turmush tarzini qabul qildi.

words.definition: A disease or period of being unwell.
words.definitionUz: Kasallik — kasallik yoki yomon holatdagi davr.
words.example1: She missed work due to illness.
words.example1Translation: U kasallik tufayli ishni o‘tkazib yubordi.
extraExamples: He recovered from a serious illness.
Illness can be caused by viruses.
She visited the doctor for her illness.
extraExampleTranslations: Kasallik viruslar tufayli paydo bo‘lishi mumkin.
Surunkali kasallik doimiy parvarish talab qiladi.

words.definition: The process of returning to health after illness or injury.
words.definitionUz: Tuzalish — kasallik yoki jarohatdan keyin sog‘lig‘ini tiklash jarayoni.
words.example1: Her recovery was quick.
words.example3: He is in recovery after surgery.
extraExamples: The doctor is happy with her recovery.

words.definition: Treatment for a health problem, often talking with a specialist.
words.definitionUz: Terapiya — sog‘liq muammosini davolash, ko‘pincha mutaxassis bilan suhbatlashish.
words.example1: She is going to therapy.
words.example2Translation: Jismoniy terapiya uning jarohatiga yordam berdi.
extraExampleTranslations: San’at terapiyasi his-tuyg‘ularni ifodalashga yordam beradi.

words.definition: A person who studies and treats mental processes and behavior.
words.definitionUz: Psixolog — ruhiy jarayonlar va xatti-harakatni o‘rganadigan va davolaydigan mutaxassis.
words.example1: She visits a psychologist regularly.
words.example2: The psychologist helped him with anxiety.
words.example2Translation: Psixolog unga tashvish bilan kurashishda yordam berdi.
words.example3: Psychologists work in schools and hospitals.
extraExamples: He wants to become a psychologist.
Psychologists help with mental health issues.
The session with the psychologist was helpful.
extraExampleTranslations: Psixologlar ruhiy salomatlik muammolarida yordam berishadi.
Ko‘p talabalar maktab psixologlariga murojaat qilishadi.

words.definition: A person who gives advice on food and nutrition.
words.definitionUz: Ovqatlanish mutaxassisi — ovqat va ovqatlanish bo‘yicha maslahat beruvchi shaxs.
words.example1: The nutritionist created a meal plan.
words.example2: She consulted a nutritionist for better health.
words.example2Translation: U sog‘lig‘ini yaxshilash uchun ovqatlanish mutaxassisiga murojaat qildi.
words.example3: Nutritionists work in hospitals and gyms.
words.example3Translation: Ovqatlanish mutaxassislari shifoxona va sport zallarida ishlaydi.
extraExamples: He met with a nutritionist weekly.
She wants to become a nutritionist.
The nutritionist explained the importance of balanced diet.
A good nutritionist provides practical advice.
extraExampleTranslations: Ovqatlanish mutaxassisi ko‘proq sabzavot tavsiya qildi.
U har hafta ovqatlanish mutaxassisi bilan uchrashadi.
Ovqatlanish mutaxassislari vaznni boshqarishda yordam berishadi.
Ovqatlanish mutaxassisi muvozanatli ovqatlanish muhimligini tushuntirdi.
Ko‘p sportchilar ovqatlanish mutaxassislari bilan ishlaydi.
Yaxshi ovqatlanish mutaxassisi amaliy maslahat beradi.

words.definition: The state of being healthy, happy, and comfortable.
words.definitionUz: Farovonlik — sog‘lom, baxtli va qulay holatda bo‘lish.
words.example1: Wellbeing is important for everyone.
words.example2: The program focuses on employee wellbeing.
words.example3Translation: Ruhiy farovonlik kundalik hayotga ta’sir qiladi.
extraExamples: The company cares about staff wellbeing.
Wellbeing includes physical and mental health.
She works on her personal wellbeing.
extraExampleTranslations: Farovonlik jismoniy va ruhiy salomatlikni o‘z ichiga oladi.

words.definition: Services provided to maintain or improve health.
words.definitionUz: Tibbiy yordam — salomatlikni saqlash yoki yaxshilash uchun ko‘rsatiladigan xizmatlar.
words.example1: Good healthcare is essential.
words.example2: Access to healthcare varies by country.
words.example2Translation: Tibbiy yordamga kirish mamlakatga qarab farq qiladi.
extraExampleTranslations: Pandemiya paytida tibbiy yordam juda muhim.

words.definition: The condition of being very overweight.
words.definitionUz: Semizlik — juda ortiqcha vaznga ega bo‘lish holati.
words.example1: Obesity is a growing problem.
extraExamples: Obesity rates are high in some countries.
She overcame obesity through lifestyle changes.
extraExampleTranslations: U turmush tarzini o‘zgartirib semizlikdan qutuldi.

words.definition: A widespread occurrence of an infectious disease.
words.definitionUz: Epidemiya — yuqumli kasallikning keng tarqalishi.
words.example1: The country faced an epidemic.
words.example3Translation: Jamoat salomatligi choralari epidemiyalarni nazorat qiladi.
extraExamples: The flu epidemic affected many people.
extraExampleTranslations: Gripp epidemiyasi ko‘p odamlarga ta’sir qildi.

words.definition: A traditional way of behaving or doing something.
words.definitionUz: Ur-f-odat — biror narsani bajarish yoki o‘zini tutishning an’anaviy usuli.
words.example1: It is a custom to shake hands.
words.example3: Wedding customs vary around the world.
words.example3Translation: To‘y urf-odatlari dunyo bo‘ylab farq qiladi.
extraExamples: Customs differ from culture to culture.
The custom is to remove shoes indoors.
extraExampleTranslations: Uy ichida oyoq kiyimini yechish urf-odati.

words.definition: A group of people living in the same place or having something in common.
words.definitionUz: Community - bir joyda yashaydigan yoki biror narsada umumiy jihatlari bo‘lgan odamlar guruhi.
words.example1: The local community helped clean the park.
words.example1Translation: Mahalliy jamiyat parkni tozalashda yordam berdi.
words.example2: She is very active in her community.
words.example3: The school is an important part of the community.
extraExamples: The online community is very supportive.
Community service is required for the program.
He feels a strong sense of community.
The community center offers many activities.
Problems are solved together by the community.
This decision affects the whole community.
extraExampleTranslations: Dastur uchun jamiyatga xizmat talab qilinadi.
U kuchli jamiyat tuyg‘usini his qiladi.
Jamiyat markazi ko‘p tadbirlarni taklif qiladi.
Muammolar jamiyat tomonidan birgalikda hal qilinadi.
Bu qaror butun jamiyatga ta'sir qiladi.

words.definition: The difference in opinions, values, and attitudes between older and younger people.
words.definitionUz: Generation gap - katta yoshdagilar va yoshlar o‘rtasidagi fikr, qadriyat va munosabatdagi farq.
words.example1: The generation gap makes it hard for parents and children to understand each other.
words.example1Translation: Avlodlar tafovuti ota-onalar va bolalar o‘rtasida tushunishni qiyinlashtiradi.
words.example2: Technology has widened the generation gap.
words.example3: There is always a generation gap in society.
words.example3Translation: Jamiyatda har doim avlodlar tafovuti mavjud.
extraExamples: They tried to bridge the generation gap.
Music is often a cause of generation gap.
The generation gap is smaller in some families.
He wrote an essay about the generation gap.
Understanding the generation gap is important.
Fashion creates a visible generation gap.
The generation gap affects workplace communication.
extraExampleTranslations: Ular avlodlar tafovutini bartaraf etishga harakat qilishdi.
Musiqa ko‘pincha avlodlar tafovutiga sabab bo‘ladi.
U avlodlar tafovuti haqida insho yozgan.
Moda avlodlar tafovutini ko‘zga ko‘rinadigan qiladi.
Avlodlar tafovuti ish joyidagi muloqotga ta'sir qiladi.

words.definition: The state of being equal, especially in rights and opportunities.
words.definitionUz: Equality - huquq va imkoniyatlarda teng bo‘lish holati.
words.example1: The movement fights for gender equality.
words.example1Translation: Bu harakat gender tengligi uchun kurashadi.
words.example2: Equality is an important value in society.
words.example3: We need more equality in education.
extraExamples: Everyone deserves equality before the law.
The company promotes equality in the workplace.
Racial equality is still a goal.
True equality has not yet been achieved.
Equality between rich and poor is difficult.
extraExampleTranslations: Hamma qonun oldida teng huquqqa loyiq.
Boy va kambag‘allar o‘rtasidagi tenglik qiyin.

words.definition: Unfair treatment of people because of their race, gender, age, etc.
words.definitionUz: Discrimination - odamlarni irq, jins, yosh va hokazolarga qarab adolatsiz muomala qilish.
words.example1: Discrimination in the workplace is illegal.
words.example2: She faced discrimination because of her religion.
words.example2Translation: U dini tufayli kamsitishga duch keldi.
words.example3: We must fight against all forms of discrimination.
words.example3Translation: Biz har qanday kamsitishga qarshi kurashishimiz kerak.
extraExamples: Racial discrimination is a serious problem.
The law protects against gender discrimination.

words.definition: The state of being extremely poor.
words.definitionUz: Poverty - juda kambag‘al bo‘lish holati.
words.example1: Many organizations fight against poverty.
words.example3: The government is trying to reduce poverty.
extraExamples: Extreme poverty still exists in some regions.
Poverty leads to many social problems.
They donated money to fight poverty.
Education is a way out of poverty.
Living in poverty is very difficult.
extraExampleTranslations: Ba'zi mintaqalarda o‘ta qashshoqlik hali ham mavjud.
Qashshoqlik ko‘p ijtimoiy muammolarga olib keladi.
Ular qashshoqlikka qarshi kurash uchun pul ehson qildilar.

words.definition: A large amount of money or valuable possessions.
words.definitionUz: Wealth - katta miqdordagi pul yoki qimmatbaho buyumlar.
words.example1: He built his wealth through hard work.
words.example1Translation: U boyligini qattiq mehnat bilan yaratdi.
words.example2: The country has great natural wealth.
words.example3: Wealth does not always bring happiness.
words.example3Translation: Boylik har doim ham baxt keltirmaydi.
extraExamples: The distribution of wealth is unequal.
She inherited a lot of wealth.
Education is a form of wealth.
The family's wealth comes from business.
Wealth brings both opportunities and responsibilities.
He donated part of his wealth to charity.
True wealth is in health and relationships.
extraExampleTranslations: U ko‘p boylikni meros qilib oldi.
U boyligining bir qismini xayriyaga berdi.

words.definition: The process by which businesses, ideas, and cultures spread around the world.
words.definitionUz: Globalisation - biznes, g‘oyalar va madaniyatlar butun dunyoga tarqalish jarayoni.
words.example1: Globalisation has changed the way we trade.
words.example2: Thanks to globalisation we can enjoy food from many countries.
words.example2Translation: Globallashuv tufayli biz ko‘p mamlakatlar ovqatidan bahramand bo‘lishimiz mumkin.
words.example3: The effects of globalisation are both positive and negative.
words.example3Translation: Globallashuvning ta'siri ham ijobiy, ham salbiy.
extraExamples: We live in an age of globalisation.

words.definition: All the people living in a particular country or area.
words.definitionUz: Population - muayyan mamlakat yoki hududda yashaydigan barcha odamlar.
words.example1: The population of the city is growing rapidly.
words.example2: China has the largest population in the world.
words.example2Translation: Xitoy dunyodagi eng katta aholiga ega.
words.example3: Population growth is a global issue.
extraExamples: The aging population needs more care.
The population of this village is small.
extraExampleTranslations: Aholining haddan tashqari ko‘pligi ko‘p muammolarga olib keladi.
Qariyib borayotgan aholiga ko‘proq g‘amxo‘rlik kerak.
Ta'lim aholining o‘sishini boshqarishga yordam beradi.

words.definition: Relating to a town or city.
words.definitionUz: Urban - shahar yoki shaharchaga tegishli.
words.example1: Urban areas have better access to services.
words.example2: She prefers urban life to rural life.
words.example2Translation: U qishloq hayotidan ko‘ra shahar hayotini afzal ko‘radi.
words.example3: Urban development is changing the landscape.
extraExamples: Urban problems include traffic and pollution.
Many young people move to urban centers.
There is a big difference between urban and rural areas.
extraExampleTranslations: Shahar muammolari orasida tirbandlik va ifloslanish bor.
Ko‘p yoshlar shahar markazlariga ko‘chib o‘tadi.
Shahar va qishloq hududlari o‘rtasida katta farq bor.

words.definition: Relating to the countryside.
words.definitionUz: Rural - qishloq joylariga tegishli.
words.example1: Rural areas often have beautiful nature.
words.example1Translation: Qishloq hududlari ko‘pincha go‘zal tabiatga ega.
words.example2: Life in rural communities is slower.
words.example3: Many rural schools need better facilities.
words.example3Translation: Ko‘p qishloq maktablariga yaxshiroq sharoit kerak.
extraExamples: Rural development is a government priority.
He grew up in a rural village.
Access to healthcare is limited in rural areas.
There is a big urban-rural divide.
extraExampleTranslations: Qishloq hududlarida tibbiy yordam olish cheklangan.
Shahar va qishloq o‘rtasida katta tafovut bor.

words.definition: Newspapers, television, radio, and the internet used to communicate information.
words.definitionUz: Media - ma'lumot tarqatish uchun gazeta, televideniye, radio va internet.
words.example1: Social media has changed how we communicate.
words.example1Translation: Ijtimoiy media bizning muloqot usulimizni o‘zgartirdi.
words.example2: The media reported the news quickly.
words.example2Translation: Ommaviy axborot vositalari yangilikni tez tarqatdi.
words.example3: She works in the media industry.
extraExamples: He follows the news on different media.
Social media platforms are very popular.
Media plays an important role in democracy.

words.definition: Something that is usual or standard in a society.
words.definitionUz: Norm - jamiyatda odatiy yoki standart bo‘lgan narsa.
words.example1: It is the norm to shake hands when meeting someone.
words.example1Translation: Kimdir bilan uchrashganda qo‘l siqish norma hisoblanadi.
words.example2: Working from home became the new norm.
words.example3Translation: Ijtimoiy normalar madaniyatlar o‘rtasida farq qiladi.
extraExamples: Punctuality is the norm in this company.
Breaking social norms can be difficult.
This behavior is not the norm.
The norm is to study hard in school.
New norms are developing after the pandemic.
extraExampleTranslations: Ijtimoiy normalarni buzish qiyin bo‘lishi mumkin.
Madaniy normalar bizning harakatlarimizga ta'sir qiladi.

words.definition: The way someone acts or behaves.
words.definitionUz: Behaviour - odamning harakat qilish yoki o‘zini tutish usuli.
words.example1: His behaviour improved after the talk.
words.example1Translation: U bilan suhbatdan keyin uning xulq-atvori yaxshilandi.
words.example2: Good behaviour is rewarded at school.
words.example3: We need to change our consumer behaviour.
extraExamples: Bad behaviour will not be tolerated.
Animal behaviour is interesting to study.

words.definition: The combination of qualities that makes a person different from others.
words.definitionUz: Personality - odamni boshqalardan farqlovchi sifatlar majmui.
words.example1: She has a very friendly personality.
words.example2: Personality is more important than appearance.
words.example3: His strong personality helped him succeed.
words.example3Translation: Uning kuchli shaxsiyati muvaffaqiyat qozonishga yordam berdi.
extraExamples: The job requires a certain personality.
She developed her personality through travel.
Personality traits can change over time.
The teacher has a great personality.
extraExampleTranslations: Shaxsiyat xususiyatlari vaqt o‘tishi bilan o‘zgarishi mumkin.

words.definition: The mental and moral qualities of a person.
words.definitionUz: Character - odamning ruhiy va axloqiy sifatlari.
words.example1: He is a man of strong character.
words.example3: Good character is built over time.
words.example3Translation: Yaxshi xarakter vaqt o‘tishi bilan shakllanadi.
extraExamples: Honesty is an important character trait.
The main character in the movie was brave.
Character development is important in education.
He showed his true character in difficult times.
The book describes many different characters.
extraExampleTranslations: U qiyin paytlarda haqiqiy xarakterini ko‘rsatdi.
Kitobda ko‘p turli xil personajlar tasvirlangan.

words.definition: The activities associated with governing a country or area.
words.definitionUz: Politics - mamlakat yoki hududni boshqarish bilan bog‘liq faoliyat.
words.example1: He is very interested in politics.
words.example3Translation: Mahalliy siyosat kundalik hayotga ta'sir qiladi.
extraExamples: The politics of the country are changing.
He entered politics at a young age.
The book is about the history of politics.
extraExampleTranslations: Siyosat yaxshi muloqot ko‘nikmalarini talab qiladi.

words.definition: The system by which a country or area produces, manages, and uses its money and goods.
words.definitionUz: Economy - mamlakat yoki hududning pul va tovarlarni ishlab chiqarish, boshqarish va ishlatish tizimi.
words.example1: The economy is growing this year.
words.example2: Tourism is important for the local economy.
extraExamples: The government is trying to improve the economy.
The country has a mixed economy.
Economic problems can lead to unrest.
She works in the banking sector of the economy.
The economy recovered after the crisis.
extraExampleTranslations: Iqtisodiy muammolar tartibsizlikka olib kelishi mumkin.

words.definition: The way in which two or more people or things are connected.
words.definitionUz: Relationship - ikki yoki undan ortiq odam yoki narsalar o‘rtasidagi bog‘lanish usuli.
words.example1: They have a very good relationship.
words.example3: The relationship between the two countries is improving.
extraExamples: She is in a long-term relationship.
Good communication is key to any relationship.
The teacher has a great relationship with students.
extraExampleTranslations: Yaxshi muloqot har qanday munosabatning kalitidir.

words.definition: The state of being friends with someone.
words.definitionUz: Friendship - kimdir bilan do‘st bo‘lish holati.
words.example1: Their friendship has lasted for many years.
words.example1Translation: Ularning do‘stligi ko‘p yillardan beri davom etmoqda.
words.example3: She values friendship more than anything.
words.example3Translation: U do‘stlikni hamma narsadan ustun qo‘yadi.
extraExamples: Old friendships are important to keep.
Friendship can develop at any age.
extraExampleTranslations: Do‘stlik ishonch va halollikni talab qiladi.
Do‘stlik har qanday yoshda rivojlanishi mumkin.

words.definition: Relating to society or life in a community.
words.definitionUz: Social - jamiyat yoki jamoaviy hayotga tegishli.
words.example1: Social skills are important for success.
words.example3: Social issues are discussed in the meeting.
extraExamples: The party was a social event.
He has a strong social circle.
Social workers help people in need.
Social interaction is good for mental health.
extraExampleTranslations: Ijtimoiy xodimlar muhtoj odamlarga yordam beradi.
Ijtimoiy muloqot ruhiy salomatlik uchun foydali.

words.definition: Relating to or shared by all people in society.
words.definitionUz: Public - jamiyatdagi barcha odamlar tomonidan baham ko‘riladigan yoki ularga tegishli.
words.example1: The park is open to the public.
extraExamples: The meeting is open to the public.
The library is a public building.

words.definition: Relating to a particular area or place.
words.definitionUz: Local - muayyan hudud yoki joyga tegishli.
words.example1: We buy food from local farmers.
words.example1Translation: Biz mahalliy fermerlardan ovqat sotib olamiz.
words.example2: The local news is very interesting.
extraExamples: The local government made a decision.
The festival is a local event.
She knows all the local places.

words.definition: Relating to the whole world.
words.definitionUz: Global - butun dunyoga tegishli.
words.example1: Climate change is a global problem.
words.example2: The company has a global presence.
words.example3: We live in a global village.
extraExamples: The event had a global audience.

words.definition: Relating to a whole nation or country.
words.definitionUz: National - butun millat yoki mamlakatga tegishli.
words.example1: The national anthem was played.
extraExamples: He works for a national newspaper.

words.definition: The group of people who govern a country or state.
words.definitionUz: Government - mamlakat yoki davlatni boshqaradigan odamlar guruhi.
words.example1: The government announced new policies.
extraExamples: The local government is responsible for roads.
She works in the government office.
The government is investing in education.
Criticism of the government is common.
The government changed after the election.

words.definition: Entitlements that people have by law or moral principle.
words.definitionUz: Rights - qonun yoki axloqiy tamoyil bo‘yicha odamlarning huquqlari.
words.example1: Everyone has basic human rights.
words.example3: The rights of children must be protected.
extraExamples: Animal rights are becoming more important.
He knows his rights very well.
Voting is a basic democratic right.
Freedom of speech is a right.
extraExampleTranslations: U o‘z huquqlarini juda yaxshi biladi.

words.definition: The power to act, speak, or think without restriction.
words.definitionUz: Freedom - cheklovlarsiz harakat qilish, gapirish yoki fikrlash imkoniyati.
words.example1: Freedom of speech is important in democracy.
words.example3: Children need some freedom to play.
words.example3Translation: Bolalar o‘ynash uchun biroz erkinlikka muhtoj.
extraExamples: The country gained independence and freedom.
She enjoys the freedom of working from home.
Academic freedom is important for universities.
Too much freedom can cause problems.
extraExampleTranslations: U uyda ishlash erkinligidan zavq oladi.
Juda ko‘p erkinlik muammolarga olib kelishi mumkin.

words.definition: Fair treatment and the administration of the law.
words.definitionUz: Justice - adolatli muomala va qonunning qo‘llanilishi.
words.example1: We must fight for social justice.
words.example1Translation: Biz ijtimoiy adolat uchun kurashishimiz kerak.
extraExamples: They demanded justice for the victims.
True justice is hard to achieve.
extraExampleTranslations: Ular qurbonlar uchun adolat talab qilishdi.

words.definition: An action that is against the law.
words.definitionUz: Crime - qonunga zid harakat.
words.example1: Crime rates have decreased in the city.
words.example2: He was accused of a serious crime.
extraExamples: She is afraid of crime in the area.
The government is tough on crime.

words.definition: The unfair situation where people do not have equal rights or opportunities.
words.definitionUz: Inequality - odamlar teng huquq yoki imkoniyatlarga ega bo‘lmagan adolatsiz holat.
words.example1: Economic inequality is a big problem.
words.example2: We need to reduce inequality in society.
extraExamples: The gap between rich and poor shows inequality.
They work to end racial inequality.
extraExampleTranslations: Boy va kambag‘al o‘rtasidagi farq tengsizlikni ko‘rsatadi.
Tengsizlik ijtimoiy tartibsizlikka olib kelishi mumkin.
Ular irqiy tengsizlikni tugatish uchun ishlaydilar.

words.definition: The belief that some races are better than others, leading to unfair treatment.
words.definitionUz: Racism - ba'zi irqlar boshqalardan ustun deb hisoblash va adolatsiz muomala.
words.example1: Racism has no place in modern society.
extraExamples: Systemic racism is a serious issue.
The movement against racism is growing.
The film addresses the problem of racism.
extraExampleTranslations: U ish joyida irqchilikka duch keldi.

words.definition: The state of being male or female, especially in social and cultural contexts.
words.definitionUz: Gender - erkak yoki ayol bo‘lish holati, ayniqsa ijtimoiy va madaniy jihatdan.
words.example1: Gender equality is a global goal.
words.example3: Gender roles are changing in society.
extraExamples: Gender balance is important in leadership.
The survey included questions about gender.
Gender issues are discussed openly now.
There is still a gender pay gap.
extraExampleTranslations: So‘rovda gender haqida savollar bor edi.
Gender masalalari hozir ochiq muhokama qilinadi.
Hali ham gender ish haqi tafovuti mavjud.

words.definition: A smaller group of people within a larger group or society.
words.definitionUz: Minority - kattaroq guruh yoki jamiyat ichidagi kichikroq odamlar guruhi.
words.example1: Minority groups need protection.
words.example2: She belongs to an ethnic minority.
words.example3: The rights of minorities must be respected.
extraExamples: He is from a religious minority.
Minority representation in politics is increasing.

words.definition: The larger part of a group or the greater number.
words.definitionUz: Majority - guruhning kattaroq qismi yoki ko‘pchilik.
words.example1: The majority of people agreed with the decision.
words.example1Translation: Odamlarning ko‘pchiligi qaror bilan rozi bo‘ldi.
words.example2: She won by a large majority.
words.example2Translation: U katta ko‘pchilik ovoz bilan g‘alaba qozondi.
words.example3: The majority of students passed the exam.
extraExamples: In the majority of cases, this works.
They form the majority of the population.
The majority supported the new law.
Working with the majority is easier.

words.definition: A system of government where people choose their leaders by voting.
words.definitionUz: Democracy - odamlar ovoz berib rahbarlarini tanlaydigan boshqaruv tizimi.
words.example1: Democracy allows people to express their opinions.
words.example1Translation: Demokratiya odamlarga o‘z fikrlarini bildirishga imkon beradi.
words.example2: The country is building a strong democracy.
words.example3: Free elections are the basis of democracy.
extraExamples: Democracy is based on the will of the people.
The transition to democracy was difficult.
They live in a democratic society.

words.definition: The practice of examining and removing information that is considered harmful.
words.definitionUz: Censorship - zararli deb hisoblangan ma'lumotni tekshirib, olib tashlash amaliyoti.
words.example1: The government imposed strict censorship.
words.example3: There is debate about internet censorship.
extraExamples: Media censorship is common in some countries.
The book was banned due to censorship.
Digital censorship is a modern issue.
extraExampleTranslations: Ba'zi mamlakatlarda media tsenzurasi keng tarqalgan.
Ular film tsenzurasiga qarshi norozilik bildirishdi.

words.definition: The process of combining different groups or things into a whole.
words.definitionUz: Integration - turli guruhlar yoki narsalarni butunlikka birlashtirish jarayoni.
words.example1: Social integration helps immigrants feel at home.
words.example1Translation: Ijtimoiy integratsiya immigrantlarga o‘zlarini uyda his qilishga yordam beradi.
words.example2: Economic integration between countries is increasing.
extraExamples: The company focuses on team integration.
European integration has brought many benefits.
Successful integration requires effort from both sides.
extraExampleTranslations: Muvaffaqiyatli integratsiya ikki tomonning harakatini talab qiladi.

words.definition: The existence of many different cultures within one society.
words.definitionUz: Multiculturalism - bitta jamiyat ichida ko‘p turli madaniyatlar mavjudligi.
words.example1: The city celebrates multiculturalism.
extraExamples: Canada is known for its multiculturalism.
Multiculturalism brings both opportunities and challenges.
The festival shows the beauty of multiculturalism.
Multiculturalism is part of modern life.
She enjoys living in a multicultural environment.
extraExampleTranslations: U multikultural muhitda yashashni yaxshi ko‘radi.

words.definition: The power that social media platforms have to affect people's opinions and behaviour.
words.definitionUz: Social media influence - ijtimoiy tarmoqlarning odamlarning fikri va xulq-atvoriga ta'sir qilish qudrati.
words.example1: Social media influence is very strong among young people.
words.example1Translation: Ijtimoiy tarmoqlar ta'siri yoshlar orasida juda kuchli.
words.example2: The company uses social media influence for marketing.
words.example2Translation: Kompaniya marketing uchun ijtimoiy tarmoqlar ta'siridan foydalanadi.
words.example3: Parents worry about social media influence on children.
words.example3Translation: Ota-onalar bolalarga ijtimoiy tarmoqlar ta'siridan xavotirda.
extraExamples: Social media influence can spread information quickly.
Celebrities have great social media influence.
The study examined social media influence on elections.
Positive social media influence can inspire people.
We need to be aware of negative social media influence.
Managing social media influence is important.
extraExampleTranslations: Ijtimoiy tarmoqlar ta'siri ma'lumotni tez tarqatishi mumkin.
Mashhurlar katta ijtimoiy tarmoqlar ta'siriga ega.
Tadqiqot saylovlarga ijtimoiy tarmoqlar ta'sirini o‘rgandi.
Ijobiy ijtimoiy tarmoqlar ta'siri odamlarni ilhomlantirishi mumkin.
Salbiy ijtimoiy tarmoqlar ta'siridan xabardor bo‘lishimiz kerak.

words.definition: The way you think and feel about something.
words.definitionUz: Attitude - biror narsaga nisbatan fikr va his-tuyg‘ularingiz.
words.example1: She has a positive attitude towards life.
words.example2: His bad attitude caused problems at work.
words.example2Translation: Uning yomon munosabati ishda muammolarga sabab bo‘ldi.
words.example3: A good attitude helps you succeed.
words.example3Translation: Yaxshi munosabat muvaffaqiyat qozonishga yordam beradi.
extraExamples: Change your attitude and everything will change.
He has a relaxed attitude to work.
Attitude is more important than skills sometimes.
Her attitude improved after the conversation.
Negative attitude can affect the team.
extraExampleTranslations: Salbiy munosabat jamoaga ta'sir qilishi mumkin.

words.definition: A particular way of considering something or a point of view.
words.definitionUz: Nuqtai nazar — biror narsaga qarashning muayyan usuli yoki fikr.
words.example1: We need to see the problem from a different perspective.
words.example1Translation: Muammoni boshqa nuqtai nazardan ko‘rishimiz kerak.
words.example2: Her perspective on life changed after the trip.
words.example2Translation: Sayohatdan keyin uning hayotga bo‘lgan nuqtai nazari o‘zgardi.
words.example3: The book offers a historical perspective on the event.
words.example3Translation: Kitob voqeaga tarixiy nuqtai nazarni taqdim etadi.
extraExamples: Global perspective is important in business.
She has a unique perspective on education.
From my perspective, it was a success.
The artist painted from an unusual perspective.
Cultural perspective affects how we see things.
He gained a new perspective after talking to her.
extraExampleTranslations: Uning nuqtai nazarini tushunishga harakat qiling.
Uning ta’limga bo‘lgan nuqtai nazari noyob.
Mening nuqtai nazarimdan bu muvaffaqiyat edi.
Rassom g‘ayrioddiy nuqtai nazardan rasm chizgan.
Madaniy nuqtai nazar bizning ko‘rishimizga ta’sir qiladi.
U bilan gaplashgandan keyin u yangi nuqtai nazarga ega bo‘ldi.

words.definition: A disagreement or a reason given to support an idea.
words.definitionUz: Bah — kelishmovchilik yoki biror fikrni qo‘llab-quvvatlash uchun keltirilgan sabab.
words.example1: They had an argument about money.
extraExamples: There is a strong argument for changing the law.
They avoided arguments during the trip.
The main argument against the plan is cost.
The debate turned into a heated argument.
extraExampleTranslations: Qonunni o‘zgartirish uchun kuchli dalil bor.

words.definition: A formal discussion on a particular topic.
words.definitionUz: Muhokama — muayyan mavzu bo‘yicha rasmiy suhbat.
words.example1: The debate on climate change continues.
words.example1Translation: Iqlim o‘zgarishi bo‘yicha muhokama davom etmoqda.
words.example2: We had a lively debate in class.
words.example3: The presidential debate was watched by millions.
words.example3Translation: Prezidentlik muhokamasini millionlab odamlar tomosha qildi.
extraExamples: The topic sparked a heated debate.
There is an ongoing debate about education.
The debate helped clarify the issues.
extraExampleTranslations: Ta’lim haqida davom etayotgan muhokama bor.

words.definition: To say what you think is bad or wrong about something.
words.definitionUz: Tanqid qilmoq — biror narsaning yomon yoki noto‘g‘ri tomonlarini aytish.
words.example1: He criticised the government’s decision.
words.example2: Try not to criticise others too much.
words.example2Translation: Boshqalarni haddan tashqari tanqid qilmaslikka harakat qiling.
extraExamples: She was criticised for being late.
Criticising without offering solutions is not helpful.
The film was criticised by many reviewers.
It’s easy to criticise but hard to do better.
They criticised the new policy strongly.
extraExampleTranslations: Yechim taklif qilmasdan tanqid qilish foydasiz.
U do‘stlarini hech qachon tanqid qilmaydi.
Tanqid qilish oson, lekin yaxshiroq qilish qiyin.
Ular yangi siyosatni qattiq tanqid qilishdi.

words.definition: A feeling of uncertainty or not believing something.
words.definitionUz: Shubha — biror narsaga ishonchsizlik yoki noaniqlik tuyg‘usi.
words.example1: I have some doubts about the plan.
words.example1Translation: Reja haqida bir oz shubham bor.
words.example2: There is no doubt that she is talented.
words.example2Translation: Uning qobiliyatli ekanligida hech qanday shubha yo‘q.
words.example3: He expressed doubt about the decision.
extraExamples: There are doubts about the safety of the product.
Without doubt, he is the best player.
I doubt whether he will come.
The results cast doubt on the theory.
He has serious doubts about the project.
extraExampleTranslations: U hech qachon o‘z qobiliyatiga shubha qilmagan.
Hech shubhasiz, u eng yaxshi o‘yinchi.
U loyiha haqida jiddiy shubhalarga ega.

words.definition: Hopeful and confident about the future.
words.definitionUz: Optimistik — kelajakka umidvor va ishonchli.
words.example1: She is always optimistic about life.
words.example2: The team is optimistic about winning.
words.example3: He has an optimistic view of the situation.
extraExamples: I am optimistic that things will improve.
The forecast is more optimistic now.
They are optimistic about the future.
extraExampleTranslations: U qiyinchiliklarga qaramay optimistik bo‘lib qoldi.

words.definition: Tending to see the worst aspect of things.
words.definitionUz: Pessimistik — narsalarning eng yomon tomonlarini ko‘radigan.
words.example1: He has a pessimistic view of the economy.
words.example3: She felt pessimistic about the future.
words.example3Translation: U kelajak haqida pessimistik his qildi.
extraExamples: His pessimistic attitude affected the team.
I am not pessimistic, just realistic.
She is pessimistic about climate change.
Try not to be too pessimistic.
extraExampleTranslations: Pessimistik odamlar ko‘pincha imkoniyatlarni o‘tkazib yuborishadi.
Uning pessimistik munosabati jamoaga ta’sir qildi.
Haddan tashqari pessimistik bo‘lmaslikka harakat qiling.

words.definition: Feeling annoyed and impatient because you cannot achieve what you want.
words.definitionUz: Hafsalasi pir bo‘lgan — xohlaganingizga erisha olmagani uchun g‘azablangan va sabrsiz.
words.example1: She felt frustrated after failing the exam.
words.example1Translation: Imtihondan o‘tolmagach, uning hafsalasi pir bo‘ldi.
words.example2: He was frustrated with the slow progress.
words.example2Translation: U sekin taraqqiyotdan hafsalasi pir bo‘ldi.
words.example3: The team became frustrated after losing again.
words.example3Translation: Jamoa yana yutqazgach hafsalasi pir bo‘ldi.
extraExamples: I feel frustrated when things don’t work.
She was frustrated by the lack of support.
The long wait made everyone frustrated.
Frustrated parents complained to the school.
She tried to hide her frustrated feelings.
The job is making him increasingly frustrated.
extraExampleTranslations: U qo‘llab-quvvatlash yo‘qligidan hafsalasi pir bo‘ldi.
Uzoq kutish hammasini hafsalasi pir qildi.
Hafsalasi pir bo‘lgan ota-onalar maktabga shikoyat qilishdi.
U hafsalasi pir bo‘lgan his-tuyg‘ularini yashirishga harakat qildi.
Bu ish uni tobora hafsalasi pir qilmoqda.

words.definition: Worried about something.
words.definitionUz: Xavotirlangan — biror narsa haqida tashvishlanadigan.
words.example1: She is concerned about her son’s health.
words.example2: Many people are concerned about climate change.
words.example2Translation: Ko‘p odamlar iqlim o‘zgarishi haqida xavotirda.
words.example3: He seemed concerned after the meeting.
extraExamples: We are concerned for your safety.
The teacher is concerned about his progress.
She expressed concern about the environment.
Parents are concerned about screen time.
I am not concerned about the price.
The report raised concerns about safety.
extraExampleTranslations: Ota-onalar ekran oldida o‘tkaziladigan vaqt haqida xavotirda.

words.definition: Very surprised and upset by something unpleasant.
words.definitionUz: Hayratda qolgan — yoqimsiz narsadan juda hayratlangan va xafa bo‘lgan.
words.example1: She was shocked by the news.
words.example2: We were shocked to hear about the accident.
words.example2Translation: Baxtsiz hodisa haqida eshitib hayratda qoldik.
words.example3: He looked shocked when he saw the price.
words.example3Translation: U narxni ko‘rib hayratda qolgandek ko‘rindi.
extraExamples: The community was shocked by the crime.
I was shocked at her reaction.
They were shocked to see the damage.
He was shocked by her decision.

words.definition: To feel sorry about something you have done or not done.
words.definitionUz: Afsuslanmoq — qilgan yoki qilmagan ishingiz uchun pushaymon bo‘lish.
words.example1: I regret not studying harder.
words.example3: He has no regrets about his decision.
words.example3Translation: U qarori haqida hech qanday afsuslanmaydi.
extraExamples: Do you regret moving to this city?
They regretted not taking the opportunity.
I regret to inform you that...
No one wants to live with regrets.
extraExampleTranslations: Hech kim afsus bilan yashashni xohlamaydi.

words.definition: A strong feeling such as love, anger, or fear.
words.definitionUz: His-tuyg‘u — sevgi, g‘azab yoki qo‘rquv kabi kuchli tuyg‘u.
words.example1: She couldn’t hide her emotions.
extraExamples: The film was full of emotion.
The speech moved the audience to emotion.

words.definition: The way you respond to something.
words.definitionUz: Reaktsiya — biror narsaga javob berish usuli.
words.example1: Her reaction to the news was surprising.
words.example3: The audience had a positive reaction.
extraExamples: His reaction showed he was happy.
extraExampleTranslations: Uning reaktsiyasi uning baxtli ekanligini ko‘rsatdi.

words.definition: To think about something carefully before deciding.
words.definitionUz: Ko‘rib chiqmoq — qaror qilishdan oldin biror narsani puxta o‘ylab ko‘rish.
words.example1: I will consider your offer.
words.example3: Consider all the options before choosing.
words.example3Translation: Tanlashdan oldin barcha variantlarni ko‘rib chiqing.
extraExamples: They considered him for the job.
We should consider changing the plan.
He is considering a career change.
extraExampleTranslations: Ular uni ishga nomzod sifatida ko‘rib chiqishdi.
Qaror qabul qilayotganda atrof-muhitni hisobga oling.

words.definition: Sure about something; without doubt.
words.definitionUz: Aniq — biror narsaga ishonchli; shubhasiz.
words.example1: I am certain that she will come.
words.example2: It is certain that prices will rise.
words.example3: Are you certain about this decision?
extraExamples: To a certain extent, you are right.

words.definition: Not sure or not known.
words.definitionUz: Noaniq — ishonch hosil qilinmagan yoki noma’lum.
words.example1: The future is uncertain.
words.example2: She was uncertain about what to do.
words.example2Translation: U nima qilishni bilmay xavotirda edi.
words.example3: The weather is uncertain at this time of year.
extraExamples: We are uncertain about the results.
I am uncertain whether to accept the offer.
extraExampleTranslations: U o‘zini noaniq va asabiy his qildi.

words.definition: To make someone do or believe something by giving reasons.
words.definitionUz: Ko‘ndirmoq — sabablar keltirib, kimnidir biror ishni qilishga yoki ishonishga undash.
words.example1: She persuaded him to go with her.
words.example1Translation: U uni o‘zi bilan borishga ko‘ndirdi.
words.example2: I tried to persuade them to change their minds.
words.example3: He persuaded the team to work harder.
extraExamples: Can you persuade her to join us?
They persuaded the manager to approve the project.
It was difficult to persuade him.
She used logic to persuade the audience.
We need to persuade more people to vote.
extraExampleTranslations: Ko‘proq odamlarni ovoz berishga ko‘ndirishimiz kerak.

words.definition: To reply or react to something.
words.definitionUz: Javob bermoq — biror narsaga javob berish yoki reaktsiya bildirish.
words.example1: She responded quickly to the email.
words.example1Translation: U elektron pochtaga tez javob berdi.
words.example2: How did he respond to the news?
words.example3: The government responded to the crisis.
extraExamples: He didn’t respond to my message.
The team responded well to the challenge.
I will respond as soon as possible.
The dog responded to its name.
extraExampleTranslations: It o‘z ismini eshitib javob berdi.

words.definition: To show or express something; to think carefully about something.
words.definitionUz: Aks ettirmoq — biror narsani ko‘rsatish yoki ifodalash; biror narsa haqida puxta o‘ylash.
words.example1: Her success reflects her hard work.
words.example1Translation: Uning muvaffaqiyati qattiq mehnatini aks ettiradi.
words.example2: He reflected on his past mistakes.
extraExamples: The report reflects the current situation.
Take time to reflect before deciding.
His mood was reflected in his writing.
The results reflect poorly on the team.
We should reflect on what we learned.
extraExampleTranslations: Qaror qilishdan oldin o‘ylab ko‘rishga vaqt ajrating.
Natijalar jamoa haqida yomon fikr bildiradi.
Biz o‘rgangan narsalarimiz haqida o‘ylashimiz kerak.

words.definition: To show or say what you think or feel.
words.definitionUz: Ifodalamoq — fikringiz yoki his-tuyg‘ularingizni aytish yoki ko‘rsatish.
words.example1: She expressed her gratitude.
words.example2: He expressed concern about the plan.
words.example3: Art is a way to express emotions.
extraExamples: She expressed interest in the job.
He expressed regret for his actions.
Music helps her express her feelings.
extraExampleTranslations: Musiqa unga his-tuyg‘ularini ifodalashga yordam beradi.

words.definition: Relating to feelings or showing strong emotions.
words.definitionUz: Hissiy — his-tuyg‘ular bilan bog‘liq yoki kuchli his-tuyg‘ularni ko‘rsatadigan.
words.example1: It was an emotional moment.
extraExamples: She became emotional when she saw her family.
He tried to control his emotional reaction.
extraExampleTranslations: U oilasini ko‘rib hissiy holatga tushdi.
U hissiy reaktsiyasini boshqarishga harakat qildi.

words.definition: Based on reason and logic rather than emotions.
words.definitionUz: Mantiqiy — his-tuyg‘ular emas, aql va mantiqqa asoslangan.
words.example1: We need a rational explanation.
words.example3: Try to be rational about this.
words.example3Translation: Bu haqda mantiqiy bo‘lishga harakat qiling.
extraExamples: His argument was rational and clear.
It was not a rational choice.
The company made a rational investment.
extraExampleTranslations: Uning dalili mantiqiy va ravshan edi.
Mantiqiy fikrlash muammolarni hal qilishga yordam beradi.

words.definition: Based on personal feelings or opinions rather than facts.
words.definitionUz: Subyektiv — shaxsiy his-tuyg‘ular yoki fikrlarga asoslangan, faktlarga emas.
words.example1: Art is often subjective.
extraExamples: We need to avoid subjective judgments.
Her taste in music is subjective.
The test has both objective and subjective parts.
He has a subjective view of success.
extraExampleTranslations: Subyektiv tajribalar odamlar o‘rtasida farq qiladi.
Testda ob’ektiv va subyektiv qismlar bor.

words.definition: Not influenced by personal feelings; based on facts.
words.definitionUz: Ob’ektiv — shaxsiy his-tuyg‘ularga ta’sirlanmagan; faktlarga asoslangan.
words.example1: We need an objective opinion.
extraExamples: Try to look at the situation objectively.
The report is objective and fair.
It is difficult to remain objective.
The test is more objective than the interview.

words.definition: Worth a lot of money or very useful and important.
words.definitionUz: Qimmatli — ko‘p pul turadigan yoki juda foydali va muhim.
words.example1: This is a valuable piece of advice.
extraExamples: The information is valuable to researchers.
Thank you for your valuable time.

words.definition: Completely necessary; extremely important.
words.definitionUz: Zarur — mutlaqo kerakli; juda muhim.
words.example1: Water is essential for life.
words.example2: Good communication is essential in a team.
words.example3: It is essential to arrive on time.
extraExamples: These skills are essential for the job.
It is essential that you read the instructions.
Money is not the most essential thing.
Essential services continued during the crisis.
extraExampleTranslations: Zarur xizmatlar inqiroz paytida davom etdi.

words.definition: Important or large enough to be noticed.
words.definitionUz: Muhim — e’tiborga loyiq darajada muhim yoki katta.
words.example1: This is a significant improvement.
words.example3: She played a significant role in the project.
extraExamples: There has been a significant increase in sales.
It was a significant event in history.

words.definition: Not important or serious; small.
words.definitionUz: Kichik — muhim yoki jiddiy bo‘lmagan; kichik.
words.example1: It was a minor problem.
extraExamples: He made a few minor mistakes.
The role is minor but important.
She is a minor character in the story.
The earthquake caused only minor damage.
extraExampleTranslations: U bir necha kichik xato qildi.

words.definition: Producing the intended result.
words.definitionUz: Samarali — maqsad qilingan natijani beradigan.
words.example1: The medicine was effective.
extraExamples: This tool is effective for cleaning.

words.definition: Achieving maximum productivity with minimum wasted effort.
words.definitionUz: Samarali — minimal behuda harakat bilan maksimal natija beradigan.
words.example1: The new system is more efficient.
words.example3: Public transport is an efficient way to travel.
words.example3Translation: Jamoat transporti sayohat qilishning samarali usuli.
extraExamples: We need to work more efficiently.
The process is quick and efficient.
Modern technology makes life more efficient.

words.definition: Difficult but interesting and stimulating.
words.definitionUz: Qiyin — qiyin, lekin qiziqarli va rag‘batlantiruvchi.
words.example1: The job is challenging but rewarding.
words.example3: Learning a new language is challenging.
extraExamples: Teaching young children can be challenging.
extraExampleTranslations: Yosh bolalarga dars berish qiyin bo‘lishi mumkin.
U ko‘p qiyin to‘siqlarga duch keldi.

words.definition: Difficult to understand or deal with because it has many parts.
words.definitionUz: Murakkab — ko‘p qismlarga ega bo‘lgani uchun tushunish yoki hal qilish qiyin.
words.example1: The instructions are too complicated.
extraExamples: The machine has a complicated design.
She has a complicated relationship with her family.
The plot of the movie is complicated.
The tax system is very complicated.
He explained the complicated theory simply.

words.definition: Made up of many connected parts; complicated.
words.definitionUz: Murakkab — ko‘p bog‘langan qismlardan tashkil topgan; murakkab.
words.example1: The human brain is very complex.
extraExamples: The problem is more complex than we thought.
A complex system requires careful management.
The plot is complex but interesting.
The building has a complex structure.
extraExampleTranslations: Murakkab tizim ehtiyotkor boshqaruvni talab qiladi.
Murakkab muammolar murakkab yechimlarni talab qiladi.

words.definition: Following old customs or ways of doing things.
words.definitionUz: An’anaviy — eski urf-odatlar yoki ish usullariga amal qiladigan.
words.example1: They had a traditional wedding.
words.example2: She wears traditional clothes on special days.
words.example2Translation: U maxsus kunlarda an’anaviy kiyim kiyadi.
extraExamples: The house has a traditional design.

words.definition: Relating to the culture of a society.
words.definitionUz: Madaniy — jamiyat madaniyati bilan bog‘liq.
words.example1: There are many cultural differences.
words.example2: The city has a rich cultural heritage.
words.example3: She is interested in cultural exchange.
extraExamples: Cultural traditions vary around the world.
extraExampleTranslations: Madaniy an’analar dunyo bo‘ylab farq qiladi.

words.definition: Relating to the economy or money.
words.definitionUz: Iqtisodiy — iqtisodiyot yoki pul bilan bog‘liq.
words.example1: The country faces economic challenges.
extraExamples: Economic inequality is a big issue.
The government announced new economic reforms.
She works in the economic department.
extraExampleTranslations: Hukumat yangi iqtisodiy islohotlarni e’lon qildi.

words.definition: Relating to the natural world and the impact of human activity.
words.definitionUz: Atrof-muhit — tabiat va inson faoliyatining ta’siri bilan bog‘liq.
words.example1: We need to protect the environmental balance.
words.example3: The company has a strong environmental policy.
extraExamples: The government passed new environmental laws.
Environmental pollution is a serious issue.
We must consider the environmental impact.
extraExampleTranslations: Atrof-muhit o‘zgarishlari yovvoyi hayotga ta’sir qiladi.
Hukumat yangi atrof-muhit qonunlarini qabul qildi.

words.definition: Belonging to or affecting a particular person.
words.definitionUz: Shaxsiy — muayyan shaxsga tegishli yoki ta’sir qiladigan.
words.example1: This is my personal opinion.
words.example2: She keeps her personal life private.
extraExamples: He has personal experience in this field.
The manager made a personal visit.
extraExampleTranslations: Uning bu sohada shaxsiy tajribasi bor.

words.definition: Not possible; unable to be done.
words.definitionUz: Imkonsiz — bajarib bo‘lmaydigan; amalga oshirib bo‘lmaydigan.
words.example1: It is impossible to finish in one day.
words.example2: Nothing is impossible if you try.
words.example2Translation: Agar harakat qilsangiz, hech narsa imkonsiz emas.
words.example3: The task seemed impossible at first.
extraExamples: It is impossible to please everyone.
The mountain is almost impossible to climb.
She found it impossible to concentrate.
With teamwork, the impossible becomes possible.
extraExampleTranslations: U diqqatini jamlay olmayotganini his qildi.
Jamoaviy ish bilan imkonsiz narsa mumkin bo‘ladi.

words.definition: Probable or expected to happen.
words.definitionUz: Ehtimol — sodir bo‘lishi kutiladigan yoki ehtimoliy.
words.example1: It is likely to rain tomorrow.
words.example3: This is the most likely explanation.
extraExamples: He is likely to be late.
It is highly likely that she will win.
The most likely reason is cost.
She is likely to change her mind.

words.definition: Biror narsaning sodir bo'lish ehtimoli past bo'lgan
words.definitionUz: Unlikely — biror voqea yoki natijaning ro'y berishi juda kam ehtimoliy holatni bildiradi.
words.example1: It is unlikely to rain today.
extraExamples: It seems unlikely that he will change his mind.
Such a mistake is highly unlikely for an experienced worker.
extraExampleTranslations: U fikrini o'zgartirishi ehtimoli past ko'rinadi.
Bunday xato tajribali ishchi uchun juda kam uchraydi.

words.definition: Biror narsa haqida biladigan
words.definitionUz: Aware — atrof-muhit, voqea yoki ma'lumotdan xabardor bo'lish holati.
words.example1: Are you aware of the problem?
words.example2: She is aware of the danger.
words.example3: He became aware of his mistake.
extraExamples: We are fully aware of the situation.
Students should be aware of their rights.
extraExampleTranslations: O'quvchilar o'z huquqlaridan xabardor bo'lishlari kerak.

words.definition: Qiziqish va hayajon uyg'otadigan
words.definitionUz: Exciting — odamda kuchli qiziqish va ijobiy his-tuyg'ular uyg'otadigan narsa.
words.example1: It was an exciting game.
extraExamples: Traveling to new places is always exciting.
The final match was extremely exciting.
extraExampleTranslations: Yangi joylarga sayohat qilish har doim hayajonli.

words.definition: Qiziqish uyg'otmaydigan, zeriktiradigan
words.definitionUz: Boring — odamni zeriktiradigan, qiziqarsiz va zerikarli holat yoki faoliyat.
words.example1: The movie was boring.
words.example3Translation: U o'z ishini zerikarli deb hisoblaydi.
extraExamples: The lecture was long and boring.
Waiting in line can be very boring.
extraExampleTranslations: Navbatda kutish juda zerikarli bo'lishi mumkin.

words.definition: Hayratlanarli darajada yaxshi
words.definitionUz: Amazing — odamni hayratda qoldiradigan, juda ajoyib va ta'sirchan narsa.
words.example1: The view is amazing.
extraExamples: The fireworks show was absolutely amazing.
He has made amazing progress in a short time.
extraExampleTranslations: Olovli otashin shousi mutlaqo ajoyib edi.
U qisqa vaqt ichida ajoyib natijalarga erishdi.

words.definition: Juda yomon yoki yoqimsiz
words.definitionUz: Awful — juda yomon, dahshatli yoki juda yoqimsiz holat.
words.example1: The weather is awful.
extraExamples: The food at the restaurant was awful.
extraExampleTranslations: Restorandagi ovqatning ta'mi juda yomon edi.

words.definition: Juda yuqori sifatli va mukammal
words.definitionUz: Excellent — juda yuqori darajadagi sifat va mukammallikni bildiradi.
words.example1: The food was excellent.

words.definition: Xavf tug'diradigan, xavfsiz bo'lmagan
words.definitionUz: Unsafe — foydalanish yoki qolish uchun xavfli bo'lgan holat.
words.example1: The bridge is unsafe.
words.example2: It is unsafe to swim here.
words.example3: The area is unsafe at night.
extraExamples: Old electrical wires can be unsafe.
extraExampleTranslations: Eski elektr simlari xavfli bo'lishi mumkin.

words.definition: Foydalanish uchun qulay va oson
words.definitionUz: Convenient — vaqt, joy yoki sharoit jihatidan qulay va mos keladigan.
words.example1: The location is very convenient.
words.example3: It is convenient to shop online.
extraExamples: The new app is very convenient for users.
Living near the station is convenient.
extraExampleTranslations: Yangi ilova foydalanuvchilar uchun juda qulay.

words.definition: Ishonish mumkin bo'lgan, ishonchli
words.definitionUz: Reliable — doimiy ravishda ishonchli natija beradigan yoki odam.
words.example1: He is a reliable friend.
extraExamples: The website is a reliable source of news.
We need a reliable partner for the project.

words.definition: Ishonib bo'lmaydigan
words.definitionUz: Unreliable — doimiy ravishda ishonchli bo'lmagan, o'zgaruvchan yoki ishonchsiz.
words.example1: He is unreliable.
extraExamples: Unreliable sources can spread false news.
extraExampleTranslations: Ishonchsiz manbalar yolg'on yangiliklar tarqatishi mumkin.

words.definition: Osonlikcha o'zgarishlarga moslashadigan
words.definitionUz: Flexible — vaziyatga qarab oson moslashadigan, qattiq qoidalarga bog'lanmagan.
words.example1: She has a flexible schedule.
extraExamples: Yoga makes your body more flexible.
extraExampleTranslations: Moslashuvchan ish vaqti xodimlarga yordam beradi.

words.definition: Boshqalarga bog'liq bo'lmagan
words.definitionUz: Independent — o'z qarorlarini o'zi qabul qiladigan va boshqalarga qaram bo'lmagan.
words.example1: She is very independent.

words.definition: Yaxshi natija beradigan
words.definitionUz: Productive — vaqtni samarali ishlatib, ko'p foydali natija beradigan.
words.example1: It was a productive meeting.
extraExamples: Good planning makes work more productive.
Exercise can make you more productive.
extraExampleTranslations: Jismoniy mashqlar sizni samaraliroq qilishi mumkin.

words.definition: Kuchli bosim va tashvish uyg'otadigan
words.definitionUz: Stressful — odamda kuchli stress va asabiy holatni keltirib chiqaradigan.
words.example1: The job is very stressful.
extraExamples: Deadlines make the work environment stressful.
She found the city life stressful.
extraExampleTranslations: U shahar hayotini stressli deb topdi.

words.definition: Juda ko'p kuch sarflaydigan va charchatadigan
words.definitionUz: Exhausting — odamni butunlay charchatib, kuchini quritadigan ish yoki faoliyat.
words.example1: The journey was exhausting.
extraExamples: Taking care of small children can be exhausting.
The long meeting was mentally exhausting.
extraExampleTranslations: Kichik bolalarga qarash juda charchatishi mumkin.

words.definition: Foyda keltiradigan
words.definitionUz: Beneficial — sog'liq, bilim yoki holatga ijobiy ta'sir ko'rsatadigan.
words.example1: Exercise is beneficial.
extraExamples: Reading is beneficial for the mind.

words.definition: Ko'pchilikka tanish bo'lgan
words.definitionUz: Well-known — ko'p odamlar biladigan va tan oladigan mashhur shaxs yoki narsa.
words.example1: He is a well-known actor.
extraExamples: Paris is a well-known tourist destination.
She is well-known for her kindness.

words.definition: Pul va moliya bilan bog'liq
words.definitionUz: Financial — pul, daromad, xarajat va iqtisodiy masalalar bilan bog'liq.
words.example1: Financial problems are common.
extraExamples: Financial planning is important for the future.

words.definition: Siyosat bilan bog'liq
words.definitionUz: Political — davlat boshqaruvi, partiyalar va siyosiy jarayonlar bilan bog'liq.
words.example1: Political news is interesting.

words.definition: Tibbiyot va sog'liq bilan bog'liq
words.definitionUz: Medical — kasalliklar, davolash va sog'liqni saqlash bilan bog'liq.
words.example1: He needs medical help.
words.example3: She works in a medical center.
extraExamples: The medical team saved many lives.
extraExampleTranslations: Tibbiy jamoa ko'p odamlarning hayotini saqlab qoldi.

words.definition: Tarixga oid
words.definitionUz: Historical — o'tmishdagi voqealar, shaxslar yoki davrlar bilan bog'liq.
words.example1: This is a historical building.
extraExamples: The museum has many historical artifacts.

words.definition: Tana va jism bilan bog'liq
words.definitionUz: Physical — tana, kuch va jismoniy harakatlar bilan bog'liq.
words.example1: Physical exercise is important.
extraExamples: She is a physical education teacher.
extraExampleTranslations: Jismoniy sog'liq ruhiy salomatlikka ta'sir qiladi.

words.definition: Aql va ruhiyat bilan bog'liq
words.definitionUz: Mental — fikrlash, his-tuyg'u va ruhiy holat bilan bog'liq.
words.example1: Mental health is important.
extraExamples: She provides mental support to patients.

words.definition: Muayyan va batafsil
words.definitionUz: Specific — umumiy emas, balki muayyan bir narsaga qaratilgan va aniq.
words.example1: Give me specific examples.
words.example3: This medicine is for a specific disease.

words.definition: Barchaga tegishli, aniq bo'lmagan
words.definitionUz: General — batafsil emas, umumiy va keng qamrovli.
words.example1: This is a general rule.

words.definition: Tabiatda mavjud bo'lgan, sun'iy bo'lmagan
words.definitionUz: Natural — tabiat tomonidan yaratilgan, sun'iy aralashuvsiz.
words.example1: She has natural beauty.
extraExamples: The disaster was a natural phenomenon.

words.definition: Yangi g'oyalar yaratishga qodir
words.definitionUz: Creative — yangi va original g'oyalar, san'at asarlari yaratish qobiliyatiga ega.
words.example1: She is very creative.
extraExamples: The artist has a creative mind.
extraExampleTranslations: Ijodiy fikrlash muammolarni hal qilishga yordam beradi.

words.definition: Mantiqqa asoslangan
words.definitionUz: Logical — mantiqiy fikrlash va izchillikka asoslangan.
words.example1: Your answer is logical.
extraExamples: The explanation was clear and logical.
We need a logical approach to this problem.

words.definition: Mantiqiy va adolatli
words.definitionUz: Reasonable — oqilona, adolatli va qabul qilinadigan darajada.
words.example1: The price is reasonable.

words.definition: O'z vazifalari uchun javobgar
words.definitionUz: Responsible — topshiriq va vazifalarni bajarishda mas'uliyatni his qiladigan.
words.example1: He is very responsible.
words.example2: Parents are responsible for their children.
extraExamples: A responsible citizen follows the rules.
The manager is responsible for the team.

words.definition: Aniq bir narsaga tegishli
words.definitionUz: Particular — boshqalardan ajralib turadigan muayyan va alohida holat.
words.example1: In this particular case...
words.example2: She is very particular about food.
words.example3: I don't like this particular color.

words.definition: Odatda bo'ladigan, xos
words.definitionUz: Typical — bir tur yoki guruh uchun xos va odatiy bo'lgan.
words.example1: This is typical weather for summer.
extraExamples: A typical day starts at 7 o'clock.
This behavior is typical of children.

words.definition: Bir xil huquq va imkoniyatlarga ega
words.definitionUz: Equal — miqdor, huquq yoki holat jihatidan bir-biriga teng.
words.example1: All people are equal.
extraExamples: The two sides have equal chances.

words.definition: Qonunga muvofiq
words.definitionUz: Legal — qonunlarga mos keladigan va ruxsat etilgan.
words.example1: It is legal to drive at 18.

words.definition: Yuqori malakali mutaxassisga xos
words.definitionUz: Professional — yuqori darajadagi malaka va xulq-atvorga ega.
words.example1: She is a professional doctor.
words.example3: He acted in a professional way.
extraExampleTranslations: Professional sportchilar har kuni mashq qilishadi.

words.definition: Turli mamlakatlar o'rtasidagi
words.definitionUz: International — bir necha mamlakatlarni qamrab oladigan yoki xalqaro miqyosda.
words.example1: This is an international company.
extraExamples: She works for an international organization.

words.definition: Yomon yoki ijobiy bo'lmagan
words.definitionUz: Negative — salbiy ta'sir ko'rsatadigan, yomon yoki ijobiy bo'lmagan.
words.example1: He has a negative attitude.
extraExampleTranslations: Salbiy fikrlar sog'liqqa ta'sir qilishi mumkin.

words.definition: Haqiqatga asoslangan
words.definitionUz: Realistic — haqiqatga yaqin va amalga oshirish mumkin bo'lgan.
words.example1: Be realistic.

words.definition: Ideal va mukammallikka intiladigan
words.definitionUz: Idealistic — haqiqatdan ko'ra ideal va mukammal holatni tasavvur qiladigan.
words.example1: He is very idealistic.
extraExamples: The plan is beautiful but too idealistic.

words.definition: Causing a lot of disagreement or argument.
words.definitionUz: Bahslashuvli — ko'p tortishuv va bahsga sabab bo'ladigan.
words.example1: The decision was very controversial.
words.example2: The film is controversial because of its topic.
extraExamples: The new law is highly controversial.
Controversial topics are discussed in class.
extraExampleTranslations: Kitob bir kechada bahslashuvli bo'lib qoldi.

words.definition: Connected with what is being discussed or considered.
words.definitionUz: Tegishli — muhokama qilinayotgan yoki ko'rib chiqilayotgan narsaga aloqador.
words.example1: Your comment is not relevant to the topic.
words.example3Translation: U bir nechta tegishli savollar berdi.
extraExamples: The book is relevant to modern life.
Make sure the examples are relevant.
His experience is relevant to the job.
The data is no longer relevant.
extraExampleTranslations: Bu nuqta juda muhim va tegishli.
Misollar tegishli bo'lishiga ishonch hosil qiling.

words.definition: Not connected with what is being discussed.
words.definitionUz: Tegishsiz — muhokama qilinayotgan mavzuga aloqasi yo'q.
words.example1: That information is irrelevant.
words.example3: The question was irrelevant to the discussion.
extraExamples: The point you made is irrelevant.
She kept bringing up irrelevant topics.
Keep your speech relevant and avoid irrelevant parts.
The judge said the evidence was irrelevant.
extraExampleTranslations: Nutqingizni tegishli tuting va tegishsiz qismlardan qoching.

words.definition: Correct and exact in all details.
words.definitionUz: Aniq — barcha tafsilotlarda to'g'ri va xatosiz.
words.example1: The report was accurate.
words.example3: Make sure your answers are accurate.
words.example3Translation: Javoblaringiz aniq bo'lishiga ishonch hosil qiling.
extraExamples: She is known for accurate work.
extraExampleTranslations: Uning bashorati hayratlanarli darajada aniq edi.

words.definition: Close to the exact number or amount but not completely accurate.
words.definitionUz: Taxminiy — aniq raqam yoki miqdorga yaqin, lekin to'liq aniq bo'lmagan.
words.example1: The approximate cost is 500 dollars.
words.example3: The approximate number of students is 200.
extraExamples: This is only an approximate figure.

words.definition: To succeed in doing or getting something you wanted.
words.definitionUz: Erishmoq — xohlagan narsangizga muvaffaqiyatli yetish.
words.example1: She achieved her goal.
words.example2: Hard work helps you achieve success.
words.example2Translation: Qattiq mehnat muvaffaqiyatga erishishga yordam beradi.
extraExamples: What do you hope to achieve?
She achieved a lot in her career.
It is difficult to achieve balance.
extraExampleTranslations: U kasbiy faoliyatida ko'p narsaga erishdi.

words.definition: To disagree with someone and give reasons for your opinion.
words.definitionUz: Bahslashmoq — birov bilan fikringizni farqli deb hisoblab, sabablar keltirish.
words.example1: They always argue about money.
words.example1Translation: Ular har doim pul haqida bahslashadilar.
words.example2: He argued that the plan was bad.
extraExamples: She argued in favor of the idea.
Let's not argue about small things.

words.definition: To act in a particular way.
words.definitionUz: O'zini tutmoq — ma'lum bir tarzda harakat qilish.
words.example1: The children behaved well.
extraExamples: Animals behave differently in the wild.
The machine is not behaving properly.
How did he behave at the party?
extraExampleTranslations: Yovvoyi tabiatda hayvonlar boshqacha o'zini tutadi.
U har doim muloyim o'zini tutadi.
Yaxshi o'quvchilar darsda yaxshi o'zini tutadi.

words.definition: To gain an advantage or be helped by something.
words.definitionUz: Foyda keltirmoq — biror narsadan foyda ko'rish yoki yordam olish.
words.example1: Everyone will benefit from this change.
words.example3: The company benefits from new technology.
extraExamples: She benefited greatly from the course.
Both sides can benefit from cooperation.
The poor benefit from this program.
extraExampleTranslations: Ikkala tomon ham hamkorlikdan foyda ko'rishi mumkin.

words.definition: To make something happen.
words.definitionUz: Sabab bo'lmoq — biror narsaning sodir bo'lishiga sabab bo'lish.
words.example1: The heavy rain caused flooding.
words.example1Translation: Kuchli yomg'ir suv toshqiniga sabab bo'ldi.
words.example2Translation: Stress sog'liq muammolariga sabab bo'lishi mumkin.
extraExamples: Loud noise caused damage to hearing.
extraExampleTranslations: Balandozv ovoz eshitish qobiliyatiga zarar yetkazdi.

words.definition: To focus all your attention on something.
words.definitionUz: Diqqatni jamlamoq — barcha e'tiboringizni bir narsaga qaratish.
words.example1: I can't concentrate with all this noise.
words.example1Translation: Bu shovqin bilan diqqatimni jamlay olmayapman.
words.example2: You need to concentrate on your studies.
words.example3: She concentrated hard on the exam.
words.example3Translation: U imtihonda qattiq diqqatni jamlagan edi.
extraExamples: Concentrate on one thing at a time.
It's hard to concentrate when you are tired.
The teacher told the class to concentrate.
I need to concentrate to solve this.
She finds it easy to concentrate in the morning.
extraExampleTranslations: Bir vaqtning o'zida bitta narsaga diqqatni jamlang.
Buni yechish uchun diqqatni jamlashim kerak.

words.definition: To give something (money, time, ideas) to help a cause or project.
words.definitionUz: Hissa qo'shmoq — biror ish yoki loyihaga pul, vaqt yoki g'oyalar bilan yordam berish.
words.example1: Everyone contributed ideas.
words.example1Translation: Hamma o'z g'oyalari bilan hissa qo'shdi.
words.example2: She contributes to charity every month.
words.example2Translation: U har oy xayriyaga hissa qo'shadi.
words.example3: Hard work contributed to her success.
words.example3Translation: Qattiq mehnat uning muvaffaqiyatiga hissa qo'shdi.
extraExamples: He contributes regularly to the newspaper.
Many people contributed to the project.
extraExampleTranslations: Har bir kichik harakat hissa qo'shadi.

words.definition: To give someone support and confidence to do something.
words.definitionUz: Rag'batlantirmoq — birovga biror ishni qilish uchun qo'llab-quvvatlash va ishonch berish.
words.example1: Teachers encourage students to ask questions.
words.example2: His parents encouraged him to study hard.
extraExamples: She encouraged me to try again.
He encouraged her to apply for the job.
extraExampleTranslations: U menga yana urinib ko'rishni rag'batlantirdi.
U unga ishga ariza topshirishni rag'batlantirdi.

words.definition: To take part in an activity or event.
words.definitionUz: Qatnashmoq — biror faoliyat yoki tadbirda ishtirok etish.
words.example1: All students must participate in the event.
words.example3: He never participates in class discussions.
words.example3Translation: U hech qachon dars muhokamalarida qatnashmaydi.
extraExamples: They participated actively in the project.
Will you participate in the competition?
Many countries participate in the Olympics.

words.definition: To give something that is needed or useful.
words.definitionUz: Ta'minlamoq — kerakli yoki foydali narsani berish.
words.example1: The school provides books.
extraExamples: She provided all the necessary documents.
They provide food for the poor.
extraExampleTranslations: U barcha kerakli hujjatlarni taqdim etdi.

words.definition: To need something or make it necessary.
words.definitionUz: Talab qilmoq — biror narsani zarur qilish yoki kerak bo'lishi.
words.example1: This job requires experience.
words.example2: The rules require students to wear uniforms.
words.example2Translation: Qoidalar o'quvchilardan forma kiyishni talab qiladi.
words.example3: It requires a lot of patience.
extraExampleTranslations: Loyiha ehtiyotkorlik bilan rejalashtirishni talab qiladi.

words.definition: To admire or show consideration for someone or something.
words.definitionUz: Hurmat qilmoq — birovga yoki biror narsaga hurmat bildirish.
words.example1: We should respect our elders.
extraExamples: Respect the rules of the school.
The teacher taught us to respect others.
extraExampleTranslations: O'qituvchi bizga boshqalarni hurmat qilishni o'rgatdi.

words.definition: To continue to live after a dangerous event.
words.definitionUz: Omon qolmoq — xavfli voqeadan keyin yashashda davom etish.
words.example1: Many people survived the accident.
words.example2: The plant survived the cold winter.
extraExamples: Only a few survived the shipwreck.
She survived on very little food.
Animals must survive in the wild.
The tradition has survived for centuries.
We will survive this difficult time.
extraExampleTranslations: Kema halokatidan faqat bir nechtasi omon qoldi.
U juda oz ovqat bilan omon qoldi.
Hayvonlar yovvoyi tabiatda omon qolishlari kerak.
Biz bu qiyin vaqtdan omon qolamiz.

words.definition: To be likely to do something or happen in a particular way.
words.definitionUz: Moyil bo'lmoq — biror narsani qilishga yoki ma'lum tarzda sodir bo'lishga moyillik.
words.example1: People tend to forget things.
words.example3: Prices tend to rise in summer.
extraExamples: He tends to worry too much.
The weather tends to be warm here.
Students tend to study harder before exams.
I tend to agree with you.
She tends to be quiet in groups.
extraExampleTranslations: Bu yerda ob-havo issiq bo'lishga moyil.
Talabalar imtihon oldidan qattiqroq o'qishga moyil.
Men siz bilan rozi bo'lishga moyilman.

words.definition: To say that you will cause harm or trouble to someone.
words.definitionUz: Tahdid qilmoq — birovga zarar yetkazish yoki muammo keltirishni aytish.
words.example1: He threatened to call the police.
words.example1Translation: U politsiyaga qo'ng'iroq qilish bilan tahdid qildi.
extraExamples: The boss threatened to fire him.
extraExampleTranslations: Rahbar uni ishdan bo'shatish bilan tahdid qildi.
Ular sudga murojaat qilish bilan tahdid qilishdi.

words.definition: To agree to take or receive something.
words.definitionUz: Qabul qilmoq — biror narsani olishga yoki rozilik bildirish.
words.example1: She accepted the job offer.
words.example3: I can't accept your apology yet.
words.example3Translation: Men hali kechirimni qabul qila olmayman.
extraExamples: He refused to accept the money.
extraExampleTranslations: U pulni qabul qilishdan bosh tortdi.
O'z harakatlaringiz uchun mas'uliyatni qabul qiling.

words.definition: To accept that something is true or that you have done something wrong.
words.definitionUz: Tan olmoq — biror narsaning haqiqat ekanligini yoki xato qilganingizni qabul qilish.
words.example1: He admitted his mistake.
words.example2: She admitted that she was wrong.
extraExamples: Don't be afraid to admit your faults.

words.definition: To give someone advice.
words.definitionUz: Maslahat bermoq — birovga maslahat berish.
words.example1: The doctor advised rest.
words.example2: I advise you to study harder.
words.example2Translation: Men sizga qattiqroq o'qishni maslahat beraman.
words.example3: She advised him to be careful.
words.example3Translation: U unga ehtiyot bo'lishni maslahat berdi.
extraExamples: She advised me to apply for the job.
extraExampleTranslations: U uni sotib olmaslikni maslahat berdi.
U menga ishga ariza topshirishni maslahat berdi.

words.definition: To give permission for something.
words.definitionUz: Ruxsat bermoq — biror narsaga ruxsat berish.
words.example1: My parents don't allow me to go out late.
words.example1Translation: Ota-onam kechqurun tashqariga chiqishimga ruxsat bermaydi.
words.example2: The school allows students to use calculators.
words.example2Translation: Maktab o'quvchilarga kalkulyatordan foydalanishga ruxsat beradi.
extraExamples: They allowed him to leave early.
The rules allow only one attempt.
She allowed the children to play.
Photography is not allowed in the museum.
extraExampleTranslations: Ular unga erta ketishga ruxsat berishdi.
Qoidalar faqat bitta urinishga ruxsat beradi.

words.definition: To make a formal request or to use something in a particular situation.
words.definitionUz: Ariza topshirmoq — rasmiy so'rov berish yoki biror narsani ma'lum holatda qo'llash.
words.example1: She applied for the job.
words.example2: Apply the cream to your skin.
extraExamples: He applied pressure to the wound.
The law applies to all citizens.

words.definition: To keep away from something or someone.
words.definitionUz: Qochmoq — biror narsadan yoki shaxsdan uzoqlashish.
words.example1: Try to avoid mistakes.
extraExamples: We avoided the traffic by taking another road.
She avoids talking about her past.
I try to avoid junk food.
extraExampleTranslations: Men zararli ovqatlardan qochishga harakat qilaman.

words.definition: To have something inside or include something.
words.definitionUz: O'z ichiga olmoq — ichida biror narsa bo'lishi yoki biror narsani qamrab olish.
words.example1: The box contains old books.
words.example1Translation: Quti eski kitoblarni o'z ichiga oladi.
words.example2Translation: Bu ichimlik shakarni o'z ichiga oladi.
words.example3Translation: Hisobot muhim ma'lumotlarni o'z ichiga oladi.
extraExamples: The list contains all the names.
Her speech contained many good ideas.
extraExampleTranslations: Bu dori vitaminlarni o'z ichiga oladi.
Ro'yxat barcha ismlarni o'z ichiga oladi.
Uning nutqida ko'p yaxshi g'oyalar bor edi.

words.definition: To have power over something and make it behave as you want.
words.definitionUz: Nazorat qilmoq — biror narsani o'z qo'lingizda ushlab, xohlaganingizdek boshqarish.
words.example1: You need to control your anger.
words.example3: She learned to control the car.
extraExamples: The company controls a large market.
She has good control over her emotions.

words.definition: To take something to a particular place or person.
words.definitionUz: Yetkazib bermoq — biror narsani ma'lum joyga yoki shaxsga olib borib topshirish.
words.example1: The postman delivers letters every day.
words.example1Translation: Pochtachi har kuni xatlarni yetkazib beradi.
words.example2: They will deliver the package tomorrow.
extraExamples: The shop delivers to your home.
The train delivers passengers on time.

words.definition: To plan how something will look or work.
words.definitionUz: Loyiha qilmoq — biror narsa qanday ko'rinishi yoki ishlashini rejalashtirish.
words.example1: She designed a beautiful dress.
extraExamples: She designs clothes for a company.
The room was designed for comfort.
The car was designed in Italy.

words.definition: To damage something so badly that it no longer exists.
words.definitionUz: Vayron qilmoq — biror narsani shunchalik shikastlashki, u endi mavjud bo'lmay qoladi.
words.example1: The fire destroyed the house.
extraExamples: The bad news destroyed her hopes.
extraExampleTranslations: Yomon xabar uning umidlarini vayron qildi.

words.definition: To separate something into parts or groups.
words.definitionUz: Bo'lmoq — biror narsani qismlarga yoki guruhlarga ajratish.
